item.title: AZ6621: Defence-Ready Indigenous Supplier List
item.description:
The Commonwealth of Australia represented by the Department of Defence (Defence) proposes to establish a list of Indigenous suppliers capable of undertaking construction works. 

Defence accordingly invites suitably qualified Indigenous Enterprises to participate in a request for information process.

item.title: NRD07664: Charles Perkins House Lighting Upgrade
item.description:
The lighting system at Charles Perkins House (CPH) is at end of life, unserviceable, and no longer compliant with current safety and performance standards.    

The existing lighting system is Clipsal Schneider DALI, as per the specifications in the Approach to Market document.    

The full LED upgrade must:

• Ensure compliance with workplace safety and illumination standards.

• Support the NIAA’s Net Zero targets through energy-efficient infrastructure.

• Achieve recommended lux levels across all workstations.

item.title: IHACPA/2526/E26-1345: IHACPA 2027 Conference – Professional Conference Organiser
item.description: Professional Conference Organiser (PCO) to assist in the planning and management of the IHACPA 2027 Conference.

item.title: OPC- 2026-03: Print on Demand Services
item.description: This Approach to Market (ATM) is for the provision of print on demand services to the public for legislation and other documents available on the Federal Register of Legislation website. There will be no ongoing fees or charges payable by OPC to the supplier for provision of this service, but fees will be charged to clients, as agreed with OPC.

item.title: RFT13109: Pre-employment screening services
item.description: The Australian Digital Health Agency (the Agency) is seeking responses from suitably qualified suppliers to provide pre-employment screening services, including Document Verification Service (DVS) checks, Nationally Coordinated Criminal History Checks (NCCHC) and Visa Entitlement Verification Online (VEVO) right to work checks (as required), in accordance with the Agency's Personnel Security Policy and associated pre-employment screening requirements.

item.title: SRC006427: Request For Tender for provision of furniture, warehousing and other equipment for Electoral Events
item.description: The AEC is seeking service providers to supply and manage the hire of furniture, office equipment, and warehouse operational equipment, including delivery, assembly, collection, packaging removal, reporting, invoicing, and dedicated account management during critical election periods. While the AEC values comprehensive solutions that can meet all operational requirements across all locations, it recognises the diverse business models within the industry and welcomes specialised proposals, including furniture-only, warehouse equipment-only, or alternative procurement models such as purchase and buy-back arrangements.

item.title: Health/E25-657123: Development, Delivery and Evaluation of Medicare Education Resources for University Medical Students
item.description: The Department of Health, Disability and Ageing is seeking to engage university medical schools in a pilot project to develop Medicare education resources, deliver the resources to their students, and evaluate the effectiveness of the resources and delivery methods.

item.title: CASA 26/106: Hire of Aircraft and Simulator Services Panel
item.description: The Civil Aviation Safety Authority (CASA) seeks to establish a Panel of qualified operators to provide ongoing access to simulator hire services, enabling relevant CASA officers to perform their regulatory functions as required.

item.description: The purpose of this Approach to Market (ATM) is to procure a suitably qualified supplier to deliver a Welding Aptitude Testing Program to support the ASA STA workforce initiatives.

item.title: 2025_PBM_1: Update the Patient blood management guideline for adults with critical bleeding and associated specialist services
item.description:
The National Blood Authority is seeking responses from potential suppliers to:

  1. update the Patient blood management guideline for adults with critical bleeding (the Guideline), trialling a living guideline approach. The purpose of the updated Guideline is to provide evidence-based recommendations, reflecting current evidence and best practice.
  2. as required, assist with the development of a variety of stakeholder communication and engagement materials.
  3. upon request, the Potential Supplier is to evaluate the evidence base across Patient Blood Management Guidelines: Module 2 Perioperative, Module 3 Medical, Module 4 Critical Care, Module 5 Obstetrics and Maternity, and Module 6 Neonatal and Paediatrics; and the Guideline for the prophylactic use of Rh D immunoglobulin in pregnancy care.

The updated Guideline will need to be developed in accordance with the National Health and Medical Research Council’s (NHMRC) Guidelines for Guidelines Handbook to meet NHMRC Standards, currently the 2016 NHMRC Standards for Guidelines available at www.nhmrc.gov.au/guidelinesforguidelines/standards. The Guideline must be capable of being approved by the NHMRC.

item.title: CFI-20260825: Defence Estate Capital and Sustainment Works Program Industry Briefing Notice
item.description: The Department of Defence's Security and Estate Group invites Industry to attend the Defence Estate Capital and Sustainment Works Program Industry Briefing. The purpose is to provide Industry with an update on Defence estate matters, including planned upcoming procurement opportunities across capital facilities and infrastructure projects.

item.title: ATM_2026_6786: Greenhouse and Energy Minimum Standards (GEMS) Check Testing Services
item.description: Provision of verification testing (check testing) of electrical and gas products registered under the Greenhouse and Energy Minimum Standards (GEMS) Act 2012

item.title: ANMM-20260630: Wharves Remediation Project
item.description: Remedial works to address deterioration and damage to Wharf 7 and South Wharf.

item.title: PROC-9177442: NGN Open Call (2026/27)
item.description: The 2026/27 NGN open Call is an opportunity to put forward regionally relevant development and extension investment proposals that respond to grower-identified issues and priorities and align with GRDC’s RD&E Strategy. This is an open, competitive process and not all applicants may be awarded funding.

item.description: Program 3 - Integrated disease management of Ascochyta blight (AB) and Botrytis grey mould (BGM) in chickpea, lentil and faba bean

item.description: Program 2 - Delivering improved Ascochyta blight (AB) and Botrytis grey mould (BGM) resistance in chickpea, lentil and faba bean

item.title: PROC-9177422: Program 1 – National Ascochyta blight (AB) and Botrytis grey mould (BGM) population monitoring
item.description: Program 1 – National Ascochyta blight (AB) and Botrytis grey mould (BGM) population monitoring

item.title: AZ6626: ESTS4000PH6 Facilities to support Hobart Class Destroyer Aegis Combat System Enhancement
item.description:
This is an Invitation to Register Interest for the Head Contractor package of works (Defence Head Contract (GMP)) at Navy Training Systems Centre Randwick and Defence Plaza Sydney under the ESTS4000PH6 Facilities to Support the Hobart Class Destroyer Aegis Combat System Enhancement Capability Project. The facilities project and the works will deliver fit for purpose shore-based facilities and infrastructure to support the integration, operation and training of the new systems prior to Q3 2029.

The notice for this procurement activity was released under AZ6622.

item.title: CSIRORFT2026019: Ground Maintenance Services for CSIRO Managed Sites VIC Metro
item.description:
CSIRO, through this RFT, is seeking responses from suitably qualified and experienced service providers to provide grounds maintenance services at its Clayton Precinct (Clayton, Clayton North and Notting Hill), Aspendale, and Werribee (Sneydes Road and South Road).

The Contractor shall provide comprehensive preventive and reactive grounds maintenance and landscaping services to ensure all outdoor areas at the mentioned sites are maintained to a high standard of safety and presentation.

item.title: ATM_2026_6489: Australia-Pacific Partnership for Energy Transition Delivery Centre
item.description:
DCCEEW is seeking a supplier to establish and administer a dedicated Centre that will act as the principal delivery mechanism for the Australia-Pacific Partnership for Energy Transition (APPET). 

The Centre will provide training and technical assistance to support energy transition policy and planning in Pacific partner countries.

item.title: AFMA 2025/2026-02: Supply of Chartered Vessel Services
item.description:
The Australian Fisheries Management Authority (AFMA) seeks to establish a panel of charter vessel service providers for an initial term of three years to supply the following services:

  1. Fisheries patrols;
  2. Land based disposal support;
  3. Disposal at sea; and
  4. Foreign fishing marine debris retrieval and land disposal.

item.title: ADSS/NOT/84124/1: SPA9102 Narrowband Payload Space Segment
item.description: SPA9102 LOE2

item.description: Infrastructure upgrades at Gallipoli Barracks and HMAS Moreton

item.description: Boeing Business Jets

item.title: S-EST10741: HMAS Stirling System Works, WA
item.description: HMAS Stirling System Works

item.title: 24/1281/41-P1: 65 Martin Place Redevelopment -Stage 2 Operational Readiness Integration Lead (ORIL)– Services
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia ABN 50 008 559 486 (the Bank), requires Operations Readiness Integration Lead (ORIL) services for the Base Building Design, 65MP Stage 2 Project.

item.title: PROC-9177443: Grain Automate Extension Program 27-28
item.description: GRDC is seeking a suitably qualified delivery partner to design, coordinate and implement the next phase of the Grain Automate Extension Program. Grain growers are increasingly exposed to automation, autonomy and digital farming technologies, yet adoption is often constrained by limited readiness, capability and confidence to implement these systems effectively. Building on the successful establishment of the Grain Automate Hub and earlier extension activities, this investment will deliver the next phase of the Grain Automate Extension Program to expand national reach, strengthen regionally relevant delivery and provide growers with practical learning pathways for adoption of these technologies. Through nationally coordinated workshops, demonstrations, digital learning resources and readiness tools, the program will enhance grower capability, support informed investment decisions, and accelerate the adoption of automation-enabled farming systems that improve productivity, labour efficiency and whole-farm performance.

item.title: S-EST11070: Russell Offices North and South Gate Works, ACT
item.description: Russell Offices North and South Gate Works

item.title: HOMEAFFAIRS/2216/RFI: Aerial Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (ISR) Uncrewed Maritime Capabilities
item.description: The Department of Home Affairs (the Department), on behalf of the Australian Border Force (ABF), is seeking information regarding the capability of potential suppliers to provide uncrewed aerial systems to conduct persistent uncrewed ISR operations predominantly in the maritime domain.

item.title: PROC-16975: Request for Proposal - Leased Office Accommodation - Wollongong
item.description: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) invites Tenderers to submit a response for the provision of leased office accommodation in Wollongong, NSW

item.title: Health/E26-205921: Delivery of the National Cancer Screening Register
item.description: The Department of Health, Disability and Ageing (the Department) seeks to engage a Service Provider to deliver the National Cancer Screening Register (NCSR), as enabled by the National Cancer Screening Register Act (2016).

item.title: ASD/NOT/95382/1: FUTURE LEAD-IN FIGHTER
item.description: AIR6002 FUTURE LEAD-IN FIGHTER

item.title: PROC-9177419: Building out the Nitrogen Mission
item.description:
Australian grain growers are among the most innovative in the world but need a breakthrough in nitrogen (N) management. GRDC has been investing in new frontiers in nitrogen management for many years and is well positioned to scale up that investment in a structured, long-term, and strategic manner via the building out of the Nitrogen Mission. The recent fertiliser supply crisis exemplifies the need to do so. Specifically, GRDC has ambitions to decrease long-term (historical) N costs as a percentage of crop revenue by 50% or more, halve current N losses to the environment, and decrease emissions intensity associated with N usage by at least 20%. In the process GRDC aims to build out world-leading research capacity and science and technology innovation in N management in the Australian grains industry. The opportunity for partners is to coinvest with GRDC in delivering a breakthrough that unlocks major economic, social, and environmental benefits in the Australian grains industry, and potentially, other agricultural markets internationally. Even a 15% reduction in long-term nitrogen fertiliser costs while maintaining yields is estimated to deliver over $1 billion AUD of value to Australian grain growers through improved gross margins and reduced financial risk.  

As a partner, GRDC is one of the world’s leading grain research organisations. GRDC has a large existing research and development (R&D) portfolio in N and related areas, extensive research, development, and innovation networks in Australia and internationally, a strong balance sheet, and a long-term outlook. Australia is also an ideal market to incubate, develop, and scale world-leading technologies in N management given the culture of innovation, technology adoption, and adaptability in the Australian grains industry. The information below describes the opportunity for partners in more detail and the process involved.

item.title: JSD/RFT/76225/1: LAND 4140 Tactical Communications Network (TCN) Tactical Edge Radio (TER)
item.description: LAND 4140 Tactical Communications Network (TCN) Tactical Edge Radio (TER)

item.title: DPC-2017: Invitation to provide Day Procedure Centre Services to the Veteran Community
item.description: The Department of Veterans’ Affairs (DVA) is seeking to contract with eligible stand-alone private day hospitals (referred to as Day Procedure Centres or DPCs) to provide DPC services for DVA Entitled Persons across Australia from 1 October 2017 onwards.

item.title: PHS-16: Private Hospital Services
item.description: DVA is seeking the provision of Private Hospital services for Entitled Persons by publishing this Invitation to Provide Private Hospital Services to the Veteran Community.
